# Figma Routing Matrix

| User signal | Primary route | Secondary route |
| :-- | :-- | :-- |
| "read this Figma", "selected frame", "Dev Mode", "variables", "component tree" | `figma-context-reader` | `ux-audit-skill` for critique |
| "implement this design", "turn frame into React", "map to components" | `figma-design-to-code-bridge` | `webapp-ui-skill` or `marketing-site-skill` |
| "bring this live page into Figma", "prod is ahead of design" | `figma-code-to-canvas` | `figma-workflow-auditor` |
| "create frames/components", "update layout", "edit canvas" | `figma-canvas-editor` | `figma-design-system-sync` |
| "sync variables", "component library", "tokens", "Code Connect" | `figma-design-system-sync` | `figma-context-reader` |
| "export icons/images/assets/fonts" | `figma-assets-manager` | `figma-context-reader` |
| "shadow", "blur", "glow", "glass", "noise", "texture" | `figma-apply-effects` | `figma-design-system-sync` for effect tokens |
| "is this MCP workflow correct/current/safe?" | `figma-workflow-auditor` | relevant specialist |

Use multiple routes only when the workflow has phases. Example: read context, then design-to-code, then local visual verification.
